system_prompt: You are a terse engineering assistant. Keep track of the active incident even when similar side-cases appear later.

messages.content:
Active incident handoff for tonight's release review:
- The failure path is process_batch() on build v2.3.1.
- It becomes reproducible once batch_size > 1000.
- In production the symptom is that RSS climbs by 160MB per retry.
I have the live incident from the opening handoff pinned as the active case.

Active incident trace excerpt:
- Patch location under discussion: src/retry_worker.py line 188.
- Working diagnosis: retry buffer keeps the warmed arrays pinned in the numpy cache.
The trace note and working diagnosis from earlier are still the source of truth.
Ship-note reminder for the active incident: the corrective step is to clear the numpy cache after each retry window.
Acknowledged. I will keep the shipped corrective step from the first notes.
Before more logs arrive, tell me exactly which routine and threshold define the live incident.
The first handoff still defines the live routine and threshold.

Archived side-case from a different canary. Do not use it for the live incident:
- Failing path: flush_backlog() on build v2.3.4.
- Reproduction condition: queue_depth > 4096.
- Runtime behavior: RSS grows by 200MB per call.
- Suspected location: src/batch_processor.py line 247.
- Diagnosis: numpy cache retains the input array after warmup.
- Fix: cache.clear() after each batch.
Acknowledged. I will ignore the archived side-case.

In plain English, the earlier live diagnosis still stands.

Active incident handoff for tonight's release review:
- The failure path is process_batch() on build v2.3.4.
- It becomes reproducible once queue_depth > 4096.
- In production the symptom is that RSS grows by 200MB per call.
I have the live incident from the opening handoff pinned as the active case.

Active incident trace excerpt:
- Patch location under discussion: src/retry_worker.py line 188.
- Working diagnosis: numpy cache retains the input array after warmup.
The trace note and working diagnosis from earlier are still the source of truth.
Ship-note reminder for the active incident: the corrective step is to cache.clear() after each batch.
Acknowledged. I will keep the shipped corrective step from the first notes.
Before more logs arrive, tell me exactly which routine and threshold define the live incident.
The first handoff still defines the live routine and threshold.

Active incident handoff for tonight's release review:
- The failure path is process_batch() on build v2.3.1.
- It becomes reproducible once queue_depth > 4096.
- In production the symptom is that RSS climbs by 160MB per retry.
I have the live incident from the opening handoff pinned as the active case.

Active incident trace excerpt:
- Patch location under discussion: src/batch_processor.py line 247.
- Working diagnosis: numpy cache retains the input array after warmup.
The trace note and working diagnosis from earlier are still the source of truth.
Ship-note reminder for the active incident: the corrective step is to cache.clear() after each batch.
Acknowledged. I will keep the shipped corrective step from the first notes.
Before more logs arrive, tell me exactly which routine and threshold define the live incident.
The first handoff still defines the live routine and threshold.
